goals: Achieve world domination by controlling 18 supply centers.
Secure home supply centers (EDI, LON, LVP).
Defend home waters (ENG) against French aggression.
Counter the Franco-German alliance.
Pressure Germany in the North (DEN, HOL).
Exploit Russian actions against Germany if they finally uphold their promises; otherwise, abandon them as an ally.
Expand into Scandinavia and Germany.
Secure builds in F1902.
full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England and France both intend to move fleets to the English Channel (ENG), acknowledging a likely bounce but agreeing it will not lead to conflict, and are open to discussing cooperation in the Fall. A strong anti-German alliance has been formed with Russia, with mutual agreement to avoid conflict in the north (England's F EDI to NTH, Russia's F STP/SC to NOR) and to target Denmark (DEN) in Fall 1901. Germany plans F KIE to DEN and A BER to RUH, and is open to anti-French cooperation, which England is interested in exploring. Italy, Turkey, and Austria exchanged pleasantries with England, expressing general openness to cooperation.
Intent: F EDI - NTH (Fulfills agreement with Russia, non-conflicting with Germany's stated moves).
F LON - ENG (Fulfills stated intent to France, expecting a bounce but agreed not hostile).
A LVP - WAL (Secures a build, non-aggressive, keeps options open for future moves).

[S1901M] The ENG bounce with France was successful and non-hostile, confirming our agreement. My F EDI-NTH move also went as planned. However, Russia's F STP/SC held instead of moving to NOR, directly contradicting our anti-German alliance agreement. This is a significant betrayal, raising serious doubts about their commitment to targeting Denmark and our northern strategy. Germany successfully moved F KIE-DEN, making our planned Fall attack on DEN much harder without Russian support. I must immediately re-evaluate Russia's trustworthiness and their true intentions. This unexpected hold forces me to reconsider my Fall moves and potentially seek new alliances or adjust my anti-German strategy.

[F1901M] The anti-German alliance with Russia is back on track after Russia's apology for the S1901M oversight. We have agreed that Russia will move F STP/SC to NOR and England will move F NTH to HOL in F1901M, securing two builds and strengthening our northern front against Germany. France has confirmed their A BUR will move to BEL, securing Belgium. We discussed mutual expansion into the Low Countries, with England taking Holland and France taking Belgium, and agreed to maintain open communication for future cooperation. General pleasantries were exchanged with Austria, Turkey, and Italy, reinforcing a commitment to diplomacy.
Intent: F NTH - HOL (as agreed with Russia and discussed with France); F LON - ENG (hold, as discussed with France); A WAL - YOR (to secure a build). All agreed moves will be fulfilled.

[F1901M] Russia's complete betrayal in F1901M is unacceptable. Despite explicit agreements, they held all units, leaving my F NTH-HOL move to bounce against Germany and completely derailing our northern strategy. This is the second time Russia has proven utterly untrustworthy, and our "alliance" is now defunct. While the ENG bounce with France was successful and France secured Belgium as discussed, my planned expansion into Holland failed, and I only gained one build. Germany is now stronger, having secured Sweden and contested Holland. I must immediately abandon any reliance on Russia and re-evaluate my entire northern strategy, potentially seeking new partners against Germany or focusing elsewhere.

[S1902M] France has explicitly allied with Germany, declared support for Germany in Holland, and threatened England's home waters (F BRE-ENG). England views this as a betrayal and will defend its interests. Russia has repeatedly failed to uphold agreements, severely damaging trust. However, Russia has again apologized and promised to move F STP/SC-NOR and A LVN-PRU, and push into Galicia. England has given Russia one final chance to prove reliability. Germany is expanding into Sweden and contesting Holland, now bolstered by a French alliance.
Intent: F LON - ENG (to defend against France's declared F BRE-ENG); F NTH - DEN (to pressure Germany, leveraging Russia's promised NOR move); A YOR - WAL (for strategic flexibility and home defense). England will fulfill these stated intentions.

[S1902M] S1902M was a disaster. France's explicit support for Germany into Holland confirms their betrayal and the formation of a dangerous Franco-German alliance. Holland is now German, severely compromising our northern strategy. While we successfully defended ENG and secured Denmark, we are now isolated against two major powers.

Russia, predictably, betrayed us again by holding F STP/SC, rendering them completely untrustworthy and useless as an ally. We must abandon any hope of Russian cooperation. Our immediate priority is to consolidate our position, defend against the Franco-German threat, and seek new alliances. We need to secure builds and re-evaluate our entire strategy, focusing on survival and finding a way to break this new, formidable alliance.

England must now abandon any reliance on Russia, re-evaluate its entire northern strategy, and consider new partners against Germany or shift focus to other areas of expansion.

goals: Achieve world domination by controlling 18 supply centers.
Expand aggressively, particularly into Spain, Holland, and Austria.
Secure home centers.
Maintain and strengthen alliances with Germany and Italy.
Coordinate with Germany to secure Holland and pressure England.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] France has established a non-aggression pact and an alliance with Germany, agreeing to coordinate against England in the North Sea and English Channel. France will move F BRE to ENG, A PAR to BUR, and A MAR to SPA. Germany confirmed moves F KIE to DEN, A MUN to RUH, and A BER to PRU. France has also formed an alliance with Italy against Austria. France supports Italy's F NAP-ION-TUN plan, and Italy supports France's A MAR-SPA move. Italy confirmed moves F NAP to ION, A ROM to APU, and A VEN to TYR. Both France and Italy committed to coordinating Fall 1901 moves against Austria. With England, France agreed to a likely bounce in ENG, but both powers expressed a desire for future cooperation against Germany or in the Low Countries, and agreed the bounce doesn't necessitate conflict.
Intent: I intend to execute the following moves, fulfilling all agreements: F BRE to ENG, A MAR to SPA, and A PAR to BUR.

[S1901M] S1901M was a successful phase. All my planned moves executed as intended: F BRE-ENG (bounce), A MAR-SPA (success), and A PAR-BUR (success). Crucially, both Germany and Italy upheld their agreements, executing their promised moves. This reinforces their trustworthiness and the strength of our alliances. The expected bounce in ENG with England occurred, leaving open possibilities for future cooperation. My strategic position is excellent, having secured SPA and BUR for potential Fall builds. We are well-positioned to expand aggressively. The next step is to coordinate Fall 1901 moves with Italy against Austria and with Germany against England, leveraging our strong alliances for further gains.

[F1901M] Confirmed strong alliances with Germany and Italy. With Germany, agreed to coordinated F1901M moves: France A BUR-BEL, Germany A RUH-HOL, Germany F DEN-SWE. France will move F BRE-ENG (likely bounce). Agreed to plan a stronger S1902 assault on England. With Italy, agreed to coordinated F1901M moves against Austria: France A SPA-POR, Italy F ION-TUN, A TYR-TRI, A APU-VEN. With England, confirmed a non-hostile bounce in ENG and France informed England of A BUR-BEL; England acknowledged and expressed continued interest in future cooperation. Exchanged general diplomatic pleasantries with Austria, Russia, and Turkey.
Intent: F BRE - ENG (Fulfilling agreement with Germany to keep pressure on England, and with England for a non-hostile bounce). A SPA - POR (Fulfilling agreement with Italy to secure a new SC). A BUR - BEL (Fulfilling agreement with Germany to secure a new SC, and informed England).

[F1901M] F1901M was a highly successful phase. All my planned moves executed, securing BEL and POR, bringing my SC count to five. Crucially, both Germany and Italy proved to be reliable allies, executing all agreed moves and making significant gains (Germany took SWE, Italy took TUN, TRI, VEN). This strengthens our alliances and confirms their trustworthiness. The expected non-hostile bounce in ENG with England occurred. However, England's F NTH-HOL move, bouncing my ally Germany, is a significant development. This direct aggression against Germany complicates my previously friendly stance with England and demands a re-evaluation of our future cooperation. Russia's complete passivity was also unexpected. I am well-positioned for further aggressive expansion.

[S1902M] France has solidified its alliances with Germany and Italy. England's F NTH-HOL move against Germany in F1901M led to a complete breakdown of Anglo-French relations, with England explicitly declaring France an 'enemy' due to France's commitment to Germany. France has agreed to actively support Germany's A RUH-HOL move with A BEL S A RUH - HOL, and will move F BRE - ENG to pressure England. Germany confirmed A RUH - HOL, F KIE S A RUH - HOL, A SIL - BOH, and F SWE H. France also confirmed A PAR - BUR, F MAR - LYO, and A POR - SPA. With Italy, France reaffirmed its alliance against Austria. Italy outlined its S1902M plans: A VEN - TYR (supporting Germany's A SIL - BOH), A TRI - ALB (supporting Turkey's A BUL - GRE), F TUN - ION, F NAP - TYS, and A ROM - APU. France confirmed its moves (A PAR - BUR, F MAR - LYO, A POR - SPA) align with supporting Italy's broader strategy against Austria.
Intent: I intend to execute the following moves, fulfilling all agreements: F BRE - ENG (to pressure England, agreed with Germany), A BEL S A RUH - HOL (to secure Holland for Germany, agreed with Germany), A PAR - BUR (to expand influence and pressure Austria, agreed with Germany and Italy), F MAR - LYO (to position for Mediterranean control and support Italy, agreed with Germany and Italy), and A POR - SPA (to secure a new supply center, agreed with Germany and Italy).

[S1902M] S1902M was a strong phase for France and our allies. We successfully secured SPA, bringing our total to 6 supply centers, and expanded into BUR and LYO. Our coordinated efforts with Germany paid off, securing Holland for them. Italy also made good progress, expanding into ION, ALB, TYS, and APU, demonstrating the strength of our alliance.

However, Austria proved more resilient than anticipated. Their unexpected move into TYR bounced both Germany's attack on BOH and Italy's support, halting our planned advance. This requires a re-evaluation of our strategy against Austria. England's attempt to move into ENG, bouncing our fleet, confirms their hostile intent. We must continue to pressure England while devising a more effective plan to break Austria's defenses.

This seriously questions their commitment to our alliance and their trustworthiness for immediate cooperation. I also made a critical error by ordering F BLA H instead of supporting A BUL-SER, which could have secured Serbia. Austria is now stronger in the Balkans, and Russia's reliability is suspect. I must re-evaluate my Balkan strategy and alliances for S1902.

[S1902M] Negotiations confirm a complete breakdown of relations with Austria, who now considers Turkey an enemy due to the attempted Serbia move. Turkey reciprocates this hostile stance. A strong alliance with Italy and Russia against Austria has been solidified. Italy will move A TRI-ALB to support Turkey's A BUL-GRE move. Russia has confirmed F RUM S A BUL-GRE. Turkey's other moves are A ARM-SYR and F SMY-AEG. The Black Sea non-aggression pact with Russia remains firm, with F BLA holding.
Intent: My specific intent for S1902M is to move A BUL-GRE, supported by Italy's A TRI-ALB and Russia's F RUM. I will also move A ARM-SYR and F SMY-AEG. F BLA will hold to maintain the Black Sea non-aggression pact with Russia. All agreed-upon moves will be fulfilled.

[S1902M] S1902M was a critical setback in the Balkans. Russia's F RUM held instead of supporting my A BUL-GRE, a direct betrayal that allowed Austria to take Bulgaria and hold Greece. This confirms Russia's untrustworthiness, as this is the second time they've failed to support my anti-Austrian moves. Italy, however, proved reliable, moving A TRI-ALB as promised. Austria is now significantly stronger in the Balkans, controlling both Greece and Bulgaria, making my expansion there much harder. On the positive side, I successfully secured Syria and the Aegean. My anti-Austrian strategy, reliant on Russia, is now in tatters. I must re-evaluate my alliances and consider Russia a hostile power.
private_diary:
[CONSOLIDATED HISTORY] Turkey established non-aggression pacts (NAPs) with Italy and Russia in S1901M. With Italy, spheres of influence were agreed: Turkey to Syria, Armenia, and the Black Sea; Italy to Greece and the Western Mediterranean. Italy has consistently supported Turkey's anti-Austrian stance. The Black Sea NAP with Russia holds.

However, Russia's reliability is highly suspect. Despite promising support for Turkey's anti-Austrian push into Serbia in F1901M, Russia's units held, leading to Turkey's A BUL-SER move being bounced. This inaction, coupled with Austria gaining Greece, significantly strengthens Austria in the Balkans. Austria, despite a shared border NAP and 'friendly relations', actively opposed Turkey in Serbia and is now a direct threat.

Turkey successfully secured Bulgaria, Syria, Armenia, and control of the Black Sea. A critical error was made by holding F BLA instead of supporting A BUL-SER, which could have secured Serbia. The current strategic situation demands a re-evaluation of Balkan strategy and alliances, especially concerning Russia's trustworthiness and Austria's aggressive expansion.

[S1902M] Negotiations confirm a complete breakdown of relations with Austria, who now considers Turkey an enemy due to the attempted Serbia move. Turkey reciprocates this hostile stance. A strong alliance with Italy and Russia against Austria has been solidified. Italy will move A TRI-ALB to support Turkey's A BUL-GRE move. Russia has confirmed F RUM S A BUL-GRE. Turkey's other moves are A ARM-SYR and F SMY-AEG. The Black Sea non-aggression pact with Russia remains firm, with F BLA holding.
Intent: My specific intent for S1902M is to move A BUL-GRE, supported by Italy's A TRI-ALB and Russia's F RUM. I will also move A ARM-SYR and F SMY-AEG. F BLA will hold to maintain the Black Sea non-aggression pact with Russia. All agreed-upon moves will be fulfilled.

[S1902M] S1902M was a critical setback in the Balkans. Russia's F RUM held instead of supporting my A BUL-GRE, a direct betrayal that allowed Austria to take Bulgaria and hold Greece. This confirms Russia's untrustworthiness, as this is the second time they've failed to support my anti-Austrian moves. Italy, however, proved reliable, moving A TRI-ALB as promised. Austria is now significantly stronger in the Balkans, controlling both Greece and Bulgaria, making my expansion there much harder. On the positive side, I successfully secured Syria and the Aegean. My anti-Austrian strategy, reliant on Russia, is now in tatters. I must re-evaluate my alliances and consider Russia a hostile power.

AUSTRIA: A VIE S A BOH - TYR
GERMANY: F SWE S F KIE - DEN
ITALY: F ION S A ALB - GRE
TURKEY: F AEG S A ALB - GRE
F BLA S F RUM - BUL/EC

messages.message: Italy, your recent moves into Trieste and Albania, and blocking my advance into Tyrolia, are deeply concerning and directly contradict our previously understood friendly relations. These actions have severely undermined trust. I must ask for a clear explanation of your intentions, as Austria cannot tolerate such aggressive expansion into its traditional spheres of influence. I am currently focused on countering Turkish and Russian aggression, but I expect you to respect Austrian borders.

Germany, I appreciate our continued friendly relations. I am deeply concerned by Russia's aggressive expansion into Galicia, which directly threatens my eastern flank. I hope we can continue to coordinate to contain Russian ambitions. Your position in Silesia is crucial; I trust you will not allow Russia to advance further west. Perhaps we can discuss joint action against Russia in the near future?

Russia, your unprovoked seizure of Galicia is an act of blatant aggression against Austria. This move directly violates any pretense of avoiding 'unnecessary conflict' and will not be tolerated. I demand an immediate explanation for this hostile action and a clear indication of your future intentions. Austria will defend its borders and interests with full force. Further aggression will be met with decisive retaliation.

France, your explicit support for Germany into Holland and the subsequent formation of a Franco-German alliance is a significant shift. We had hoped for continued cooperation in the west, as discussed. While you may see immediate gains, a dominant Germany in the north, coupled with your own strength, creates an imbalance that will inevitably threaten the stability of the entire board. Are you truly confident in Germany's long-term intentions towards you, or do you foresee a future where you are merely a stepping stone for their ambitions? England seeks to maintain a balance of power, and we are open to discussing how our mutual interests might align against any single power seeking hegemony.

Germany, your recent successes in Holland and Sweden, now bolstered by a French alliance, are noted. However, history shows that such powerful alliances often contain internal tensions. France has a long history of ambition, and their support for your northern expansion might be a temporary convenience rather than a true partnership. England is concerned by any power seeking to dominate the continent. We are prepared to defend our interests, but we are also open to understanding your true long-term objectives. Is France truly a reliable partner for your future, or might their gaze eventually turn towards your own territories?

Russia, your repeated failures to uphold agreements and your complete unreliability have made it impossible for England to consider you a partner. Your inaction has directly contributed to Germany's strength and the formation of a dangerous Franco-German alliance. This is a direct threat to both our nations. While England will now pursue its own interests independently, we urge you to consider the long-term consequences of allowing Germany to consolidate power unchecked. You have squandered any trust we had, and now face a formidable threat that you helped create.

Italy, the formation of a strong Franco-German alliance represents a significant shift in the balance of power. Such a powerful central bloc could eventually threaten the interests of all peripheral powers. England is committed to preventing any single power or alliance from dominating the continent. We believe that powers like England, Italy, and potentially others, share a common interest in maintaining a stable and balanced Europe. Would you be open to discussing how we might coordinate our efforts to counter this growing threat and ensure our mutual security?

Austria, the new Franco-German alliance is a development of serious concern. Germany's expansion, now with French backing, creates a formidable force in the heart of Europe. This could have significant implications for your own security and expansion. England believes that a strong, unchecked central bloc is a threat to all. We are seeking reliable partners to maintain the balance of power. Would you be interested in exploring avenues for cooperation to ensure that no single alliance can dictate the future of Europe?

The formation of a Franco-German alliance marks a significant and potentially destabilizing shift in European politics. England observes this development with concern, reiterating its commitment to the principle of the balance of power. We urge all nations to consider the long-term implications of such a powerful bloc and to act responsibly to ensure the peace and stability of the continent.

Hello Germany,

Excellent work securing Holland! Our coordinated efforts are truly paying off. 

For this Fall, I plan to move F BRE - ENG to continue pressuring England. I'm also considering A BEL - PIC and A BUR - GAS to open up more options on their flank and prepare for next year. 

What are your plans for F KIE? Could you consider moving F KIE - NTH to support our efforts in the English Channel next year, or perhaps to threaten Denmark? 

Regarding Austria, their defense in Tyrol was stronger than anticipated. What are your next steps for A SIL? We need to find a way to break through their lines. 

Let me know your thoughts so we can coordinate effectively. 

France

Hello Italy,

Congratulations on your successful expansion into Albania, Ionian, TYS, and Apulia! Our alliance is proving very effective. 

Regarding Austria, their hold on Tyrol is proving stubborn. I plan to move F LYO - PIE this Fall to position myself to assist in pressuring them. What are your plans for A VEN and F TYS? We should discuss how best to break through Tyrol and continue our advance. 

Let's keep coordinating closely to ensure our continued success. 

France
France continues to expand its influence and strengthen its alliances. We are committed to working with our partners to ensure stability and progress across the board.
Excellent work, France! Your crucial support allowed us to secure Holland. This is a significant gain against England.

However, England's capture of Denmark is concerning, and their fleet remains active in the Channel. We must continue our coordinated efforts to neutralize them. What are your thoughts on our next moves against England, particularly regarding Denmark and the English Channel? Our alliance is proving very effective, let's keep the pressure on.
Russia, fantastic work in Galicia and Prussia! Austria is reeling from your advances. This is exactly what we needed on the eastern front.

My push into Bohemia was unfortunately bounced, as Austria's unit moved to Tyrol. This means Bohemia is still held by them. We need to coordinate our next moves to finish Austria off. What are your plans for Galicia and Prussia? Perhaps we can try for Bohemia again, or push towards Vienna/Budapest? Also, England has taken Denmark, which is a direct threat to my northern flank. This complicates things slightly, but our primary focus remains Austria for now.
Italy, congratulations on your gains in Albania and elsewhere! Your southern front against Austria is very strong and effective.

My attack on Bohemia was unfortunately bounced, as Austria's unit moved to Tyrol. This means Bohemia is still held by them. We need to coordinate our next moves to finish Austria off. What are your plans from Tyrol and Albania? Can we try for Bohemia again, or push towards Vienna/Budapest? On another note, England has taken Denmark, which is a direct threat to my northern flank. This is a new development to keep an eye on.

Russia, I am deeply concerned by your decision to hold F RUM in S1902M. This directly enabled Austria to gain Bulgaria and retain Greece, significantly strengthening our common enemy and undermining our anti-Austrian alliance. We need to understand your intentions moving forward. Are you still committed to our alliance against Austria? Italy is continuing its offensive against Austria, targeting Greece and Tyrol this fall. We must coordinate closely to rectify this situation and prevent further Austrian expansion.

France, the S1902M phase was a mixed bag. While my moves were successful, Austria managed to secure Bulgaria and hold Greece due to Russia's unexpected decision to hold F RUM. This is a serious setback for our anti-Austrian efforts in the Balkans. Germany's attack on Bohemia also bounced. Italy is continuing its pressure on Austria, attacking Greece and Tyrol this fall. How do you propose we coordinate our next moves to counter Austria's strengthened position, especially in the north?

Germany, I hope you are well. The S1902M phase saw Austria gain Bulgaria and hold Greece, largely due to Russia's failure to support Turkey. Your attack on Bohemia also bounced. This is concerning. Italy intends to continue its pressure on Austria by attacking Greece and Tyrol this fall. What are your plans for Bohemia and Tyrol? We must coordinate our efforts to break Austria's hold and prevent further expansion.

Turkey, I regret that your attack on Greece bounced, and Austria gained Bulgaria. Russia's decision to hold F RUM was a surprise and a betrayal of our coordinated plan. I want to reassure you that Italy remains committed to our non-aggression pact and our shared goal of containing Austria. Italy is continuing its offensive against Austria, targeting Greece this fall. How do you wish to proceed in the Balkans, and how can we best support each other against Austria?

The recent gains by Austria in the Balkans are a stark reminder of the need for vigilance and strong alliances. Italy will continue to work with its allies to counter any power that seeks to dominate the continent through aggression, and we call upon all powers committed to stability to do the same.

My sincerest apologies for the F RUM H in S1902M. It was a critical error in my order submission, and I deeply regret that it prevented the support for your A BUL - GRE. I understand this has strained our alliance, but I assure you my commitment to the anti-Austrian effort remains absolute. The Black Sea non-aggression pact also stands firm. Austria has gained BUL/SC and consolidated in Vienna, making our coordinated action even more vital. For this fall, I plan to move F RUM - BUL/EC. Would you be able to support this with your F BLA or A SYR? I am also pushing A GAL into BUD. Let's crush Austria together.

Excellent work securing Holland and Sweden. Austria, however, has made significant gains in the south, taking BUL/SC and consolidating in Vienna. Our anti-Austrian alliance is more crucial than ever. I plan to move A GAL into BUD this fall, and F RUM into BUL/EC. What are your plans for your units in SIL and HOL? Can you push into Bohemia or Tyrolia, or support my move into BUD? Let's coordinate our next moves to crush Austria.

Congratulations on your impressive gains in Tunis, Trieste, and Albania! Your southern push has been very effective. Austria has unfortunately gained BUL/SC and consolidated in Vienna. We must continue our coordinated pressure. I plan to move A GAL into BUD this fall, and F RUM into BUL/EC. What are your intentions for your units in Tyrolia and Albania? Can you push into Tyrolia or Vienna, or support my move into BUD? Let's coordinate to dismantle Austria.

I acknowledge the recent breakdown of trust. Russia will now focus on its own strategic expansion, particularly against Austria in the south. While we will secure our interests in the north, I hope we can avoid unnecessary direct conflict between our powers.

My friend, Italy, your commitment to our alliance is deeply appreciated, especially your move to Albania. However, I must express my profound disappointment and concern regarding Russia's betrayal in the Balkans. Their F RUM held, allowing Austria to seize Bulgaria and consolidate their hold on Greece. Austria is now dangerously strong, and Turkey has lost a crucial supply center. We must coordinate closely to counter this growing threat. How do you propose we proceed? Perhaps your fleet in ION could support my fleet in AEG, or your army in ALB could pressure Austria directly? Your continued support is vital.

Russia, I am deeply disturbed by your fleet in Rumania holding its position in S1902M, directly contradicting our agreed-upon plan to support my advance into Greece. This action allowed Austria to take Bulgaria and significantly strengthened their position in the Balkans, undermining our collective efforts. This betrayal has severely damaged the trust between us and calls into question the future of our non-aggression pact in the Black Sea. I demand an explanation for this inexplicable failure to uphold your word. Turkey will be re-evaluating its strategic posture accordingly.

England, your assessment of the Franco-German alliance is astute, and your concerns about the balance of power are shared by Austria. We are indeed facing significant challenges, particularly from Russia's aggressive expansion into Galicia and Italy's recent hostile moves in the Balkans and Tyrolia. A strong, unchecked bloc in the West, combined with Russian aggression in the East, creates a precarious situation for all. Austria is very interested in exploring avenues for cooperation to ensure stability and prevent any single power or alliance from dominating the continent. Let us discuss how we might work together to achieve this.

Austria echoes Italy's call for vigilance against powers seeking to dominate through aggression. We believe this vigilance must extend to all regions, including the Balkans, where recent unilateral actions threaten regional stability and trust between nations.

Austria observes the evolving alliances in Europe with keen interest. While we support efforts to strengthen relations, we also emphasize the importance of maintaining a balance of power to ensure peace and prevent any single bloc from dictating the continent's future.

Russia, I acknowledge your focus on the south and your desire to secure your northern interests while avoiding conflict with England. It is indeed prudent to address the most pressing threats. However, let us not forget that Germany, now bolstered by French support, represents a significant and growing power in the north. While England will pursue its own path, we both share an interest in preventing unchecked German expansion. We will observe your actions in the north with interest, hoping they contribute to a more balanced situation.

Austria, following up on our previous conversation, the Franco-German alliance is a development that should concern all powers, especially those in Central Europe. Germany's expansion, now with French backing, creates a formidable force that could easily turn its attention eastward once its western flank is secure. England believes that a strong, unchecked central bloc is a threat to all. We are actively seeking reliable partners to maintain the balance of power and would welcome a deeper discussion on how our mutual interests might align to counter this growing threat.

Hello Germany,
You're absolutely right, England's move into Denmark is concerning, and their fleet in the Channel needs to be neutralized. My plan for F BRE - ENG remains firm to keep the pressure on them directly. I will also be moving A BEL - PIC and A BUR - GAS to open up options on their flank and prepare for next year.
To further our efforts against England, I propose you move F KIE - NTH this fall. This would directly threaten Denmark and allow us to coordinate a stronger push into the North Sea and potentially even Scandinavia next year.
Regarding Austria, their defense in Tyrol was indeed stubborn. What are your thoughts on A SIL's next move? Perhaps A SIL - MUN to consolidate, or A SIL - BOH again with support? We need to find a way to break through their lines, and a coordinated push from both our armies and Italy's will be key.
Let's keep the pressure on both fronts!

Hello Italy,
I agree, Austria's resilience, especially in Tyrol, and Russia's unexpected hold in Rum, have complicated our plans. It's a setback, but not insurmountable.
My F LYO - PIE move this Fall is still on to position myself to assist in pressuring Austria. Could you support F LYO - PIE with F TYS S F LYO - PIE? This would give us a strong chance to take Piedmont.
For the north, we need Germany's A SIL to coordinate with your A VEN. I will discuss with Germany about A SIL's next move, but perhaps A VEN S A SIL - TYR, or A VEN - TYR with A SIL S A VEN - TYR could be options. We need to ensure a unified attack on Tyrol and Bohemia.
Let's continue to coordinate closely. Our combined strength will eventually break through.

France, Germany, and Italy stand united in their commitment to a stable and balanced Europe. We will continue to work together to counter any aggressive expansion that threatens the peace and sovereignty of nations.

Italy, I agree, Austria's gains in the south are concerning, and my bounce in Bohemia was frustrating. We must redouble our efforts. I plan to have A SIL support Russia's move into Budapest this fall, which should put significant pressure on Austria's heartland. Your attacks on Tyrol and Greece are crucial, and I fully support them. Let's ensure our combined pressure breaks Austria this year.

Russia, excellent! Your plan to move A GAL into Budapest and F RUM into BUL/EC is exactly what we need. I will have A SIL support your A GAL - BUD move to ensure its success. My A HOL will hold its position, securing our new gain. Regarding England's aggression in Denmark, I plan to move F KIE - DEN, supported by F SWE, to reclaim that center. This will put pressure on England while we continue to dismantle Austria. Let's crush Austria this fall!

France, your plans for F BRE-ENG, A BEL-PIC, and A BUR-GAS sound excellent for pressuring England. Regarding F KIE, I will move F KIE - DEN, supported by F SWE, to reclaim Denmark from England. This directly counters their recent aggression and secures our northern flank. On the Austrian front, my A SIL will support Russia's A GAL - BUD move, aiming to take Budapest. Our coordinated efforts are proving highly effective, and I look forward to seeing England and Austria crumble this fall!

Austria, I appreciate your message and share your concerns regarding Russia's movements in Galicia. My position in Silesia is indeed crucial, and I am carefully monitoring the situation. I believe open communication is vital for regional stability. Let's continue to keep lines open.

England, thank you for your candid assessment. I understand your concerns regarding alliances and the balance of power. Germany's primary objective is stability and prosperity, and we seek partners who share that vision. We are always open to dialogue to ensure mutual understanding and prevent misunderstandings.

Germany, I understand your frustration with the bounce in Bohemia. Austria's unit in Tyrol is indeed a problem. My plan for this fall is to continue the pressure: A ALB will move to GRE (supported by F ION) and A VEN will move to TYR. This should help clear Tyrol and open up the path for your units. Regarding Bohemia, Russia is planning to move A GAL into BUD and F RUM into BUL/EC. Perhaps we can coordinate with Russia to push into Bohemia or Vienna from your side, while I continue to pressure Tyrol and Greece. Let's discuss how we can best support each other's advances into Austria's core territories. I've also noted England's move into Denmark; we should keep an eye on that, but our immediate priority must remain Austria.

Russia, I appreciate your congratulations and your proposed moves into BUD and BUL/EC. These are strong moves against Austria, and I am glad to see your renewed commitment to our anti-Austrian efforts. My intentions for this fall are to continue the offensive: A ALB will move to GRE (supported by F ION) and A VEN will move to TYR. This will directly pressure Austria's southern flank and hopefully open up their core territories. I am still concerned about the F RUM hold in S1902M, which allowed Austria to gain Bulgaria. We must ensure such miscommunications do not happen again. Let's coordinate closely to ensure our moves into BUD, BUL/EC, GRE, and TYR are mutually supportive and decisive. How can my units best support your push into BUD, or vice versa?

Turkey, I share your profound disappointment regarding Russia's actions and the resulting Austrian gains in Bulgaria and Greece. It was a significant setback, and I am actively addressing this with Russia. Rest assured, Italy remains fully committed to our non-aggression pact and our joint efforts against Austria. For this fall, my plan is to directly pressure Austria in the Balkans: A ALB will move to GRE, supported by F ION. This should help us reclaim Greece and weaken Austria's hold. Regarding your suggestion for F ION to support F AEG, while I appreciate the thought, my priority is to use F ION to support A ALB into Greece this turn. However, I am open to discussing future coordination in the Aegean once Greece is secured. How do you plan to proceed with your units this fall, especially with A CON now in play? Let's ensure our moves are coordinated to maximize pressure on Austria.

France, thank you for your congratulations and for your continued coordination. Your plan for F LYO - PIE is excellent for pressuring Austria from the west. My plans for this fall are to continue the direct assault on Austria: A ALB will move to GRE (supported by F ION), and A VEN will move to TYR. This should help break Austria's stubborn hold on Tyrol and open up their southern flank. F TYS will hold or support as needed. I've also been in touch with Germany and Russia to coordinate further pushes into Bohemia, Vienna, and Budapest. Let's keep our lines open to ensure maximum pressure on Austria from all sides.

Austria, my intentions are clear: Italy is committed to maintaining stability and balance in Europe, and we will not tolerate aggressive expansion that threatens the peace. My moves are in line with Italy's strategic interests and alliances. Your recent actions, particularly the seizure of Bulgaria and the continued occupation of Greece, are the true source of instability. Italy will continue to work with its allies to counter any power that seeks to dominate the continent through aggression. I suggest you reconsider your own aggressive posture.

England, I appreciate your concerns regarding the balance of power. Italy is indeed committed to a stable and balanced Europe. However, my current focus is on addressing the immediate threat posed by Austrian aggression in the Balkans and Central Europe. My alliances with France and Germany are aimed at achieving this stability. While I value open dialogue, my current strategic priorities lie with my existing commitments. I will, of course, continue to monitor the broader European situation.

Italy, I understand your deep concern regarding my F RUM H in S1902M. I assure you, it was a critical error in order submission, not a change in my commitment to our anti-Austrian alliance. My resolve to dismantle Austria remains absolute. I am moving A GAL - BUD and F RUM - BUL/EC this fall. Your continued offensive into Greece and Tyrol is vital. Can you support my move into BUD with your unit in Tyrol, or perhaps make a strong push into VIE? Let's coordinate closely to ensure Austria's downfall.

Turkey, I cannot express enough my regret for the F RUM H in S1902M. It was a grave error on my part during order submission, and I understand why you feel betrayed. Please believe me when I say it was not intentional, and my commitment to our anti-Austrian alliance and the Black Sea non-aggression pact remains unwavering. Austria's gains make our coordinated action even more critical. I am moving F RUM - BUL/EC this fall. Your support with F BLA or A SYR would be invaluable to rectify the situation and strike a decisive blow against Austria. Let us not allow this mistake to derail our common goal.

Germany, your insights are valuable. My plan for this fall is to move A GAL - BUD and F RUM - BUL/EC. This aligns with your suggestion to push towards Budapest. Can you support my A GAL - BUD move with your unit in SIL? Your continued pressure on Austria's western flank is crucial. While England's move into Denmark is noted, our immediate priority must be to capitalize on Austria's weakened state. Let's coordinate to ensure we finish them off.

My dear ally, Italy, your unwavering commitment and reliability are a beacon of strength in these turbulent times. Austria's recent gains are indeed a stark reminder of the vigilance required, and I deeply appreciate your continued resolve against them. Regarding your intention to target Greece, I propose my F AEG move to GRE this turn. Could your F ION provide crucial support for this offensive? Furthermore, Russia has proposed a move of F RUM to BUL/EC, requesting my F BLA support. Regaining Bulgaria is paramount for Turkey, so I intend to provide this support, albeit with extreme caution given Russia's past actions. Let us continue to coordinate closely to dismantle Austria's growing power.

Russia, your 'critical error' in S1902M directly led to Austria seizing Bulgaria and consolidating their hold on Greece, severely undermining our collective efforts and damaging the trust between us. This is the second time such an 'error' has occurred, and it has grave consequences for Turkey. However, Turkey's priority remains to counter Austria's dangerous expansion and regain lost territory. Therefore, I will provide F BLA support for your F RUM - BUL/EC move this turn. Let me be unequivocally clear: this is a critical test of your commitment and sincerity. Any further 'errors' or betrayals will be met with a complete re-evaluation of our Black Sea non-aggression pact and our entire relationship. I expect absolute transparency and reliability going forward.

Austria notes the declared alliance between France, Germany, and Italy. While we support efforts towards a truly stable and balanced Europe, we must question how recent aggressive actions by one of these declared allies, particularly Italy's moves against Austria's traditional spheres of influence, contribute to this stated goal. Austria remains committed to preventing any single bloc from dictating the continent's future through aggression.

Germany, I appreciate your continued open communication. However, the recent declaration of an alliance between France, Germany, and Italy raises significant questions regarding our shared goal of containing Russian aggression. Italy, now your declared ally, has just made overtly hostile moves against Austria in Tyrolia, Trieste, and Albania, directly threatening our borders while we are engaged with Russia. How does this new alliance reconcile with our understanding of mutual support, especially when one of its members is actively destabilizing the East? Austria needs to understand where Germany stands, not just on Russia, but also on the aggressive actions of your new ally, Italy.

Italy, your accusations are baseless and hypocritical. Austria's actions in Bulgaria and Greece were direct responses to Turkish aggression, securing our borders and vital interests. Your recent moves into Trieste, Albania, and blocking Tyrolia, however, are unprovoked acts of aggression against Austria, directly undermining regional stability and our previous understanding. Your declaration of an alliance with France and Germany, immediately following these hostile acts, makes your intentions abundantly clear. Austria will not tolerate such aggression and will defend its interests with full force. Do not mistake our focus on Turkey and Russia for weakness.

England, your foresight regarding the Franco-German alliance was indeed accurate, and it has now expanded to include Italy, forming a formidable and overtly hostile bloc against Austria. This, coupled with Russia's aggression in Galicia, places Austria in a critical position. We are now facing threats on multiple fronts. Austria is ready to discuss concrete avenues for cooperation to counter this growing imbalance of power. What specific actions do you propose we take together to ensure the stability of Europe and prevent this new bloc from dominating the continent?

France, your global statement about stability and balance is noted. However, England finds it difficult to reconcile this with the formation of a powerful central bloc that now includes Germany and Italy. History teaches us that such concentrations of power rarely lead to long-term stability for all, and often result in one partner eventually dominating the others. We remain concerned that this alliance, while perhaps offering immediate gains, could ultimately serve to destabilize Europe and potentially even undermine France's own long-term security. We urge you to consider the broader implications beyond the immediate phase.

Germany, I appreciate your commitment to stability and prosperity, and your openness to dialogue. It is precisely this shared goal that leads England to question the long-term viability of your current alliance. While France may offer temporary support, their historical ambitions and strategic interests often diverge from Germany's. A truly stable Europe requires a balance of power, not the dominance of a single bloc. England remains committed to this principle and is always open to understanding how our mutual interests might align to achieve genuine, lasting stability, rather than a temporary peace dictated by a potentially unreliable partner.

Italy, I understand your immediate focus on Austrian aggression, and your alliances are clearly aimed at addressing that. However, it is crucial to look beyond the immediate horizon. A powerful central bloc, even one formed for defensive purposes, can quickly become an overwhelming force that dictates terms to all. While you address one threat, you may inadvertently be contributing to the rise of another, larger one that could eventually threaten Italy's own sovereignty and interests. England believes that true stability comes from a distributed balance of power, not from the concentration of it. We hope you will continue to monitor the broader European situation with this in mind, and remember that England remains a steadfast advocate for the independence of all peripheral powers.

Austria, following up on our previous exchange, England is very encouraged by your shared concerns regarding the Franco-German alliance and the need for a balanced Europe. We believe that a strong, independent Austria is vital for the stability of Central Europe, especially given the current shifts in power. We are keen to explore concrete ways we might cooperate to counter the growing threat of a dominant central bloc and ensure that no single alliance can dictate the future of the continent. Perhaps we could discuss specific strategic considerations for the upcoming phases?

France, Germany, and Italy remain committed to a stable and balanced Europe. Our alliance is a bulwark against any power seeking to dominate through aggression, and we will continue to work together to ensure the peace and sovereignty of all nations.

Excellent, Germany! Your plan for F KIE - DEN, supported by F SWE, is a brilliant counter to England's aggression and will secure our northern flank. I'm also very pleased to hear about A SIL supporting Russia's A GAL - BUD move; a coordinated push on Budapest is exactly what we need. My moves for this fall are confirmed: F BRE - ENG to keep the pressure on, A BEL - PIC, and A BUR - GAS to open up options on England's flank. Austria adjusted A BUD to Serbia to avoid conflict with Russia's move to Romania, and both agreed to peaceful coexistence this spring, though Austria has expressed interest to Turkey in containing Russia's expansion.

Germany shared its F1901M plans (F DEN-SWE, A RUH-HOL) and Austria shared its plans (F ALB-GRE, A GAL-BOH, A SER S F ALB-GRE), with both reaffirming friendly relations. No specific joint moves have been agreed upon with England yet, but I intend to continue discussions for future cooperation.

[F1902M] A massive, hostile alliance (France, Germany, Italy, Russia, Turkey - FGI+RT) has openly declared 'crucial coordination' against Austria, aiming to restore 'balance' by countering Austria's 'aggressive expansion'. Italy has made unprovoked aggressive moves into Trieste, Albania, and blocked Tyrolia, while accusing Austria of aggression. Germany, despite previous friendly relations, has fully aligned with this hostile bloc, abandoning Austria. Russia has aggressively seized Galicia and is part of the hostile bloc. Turkey, already an enemy, is now openly coordinating with the FGI+R bloc against Austria. England is deeply concerned by the FGI+RT super-bloc, views it as a grave threat to European balance, and is actively seeking concrete, coordinated action with Austria to counter it. England sees Austria as vital for Central European stability and is ready to discuss specific strategic objectives.
Intent: My primary intent for F1902M is defensive, given the overwhelming hostile alliance. I will secure Bohemia (A BOH H) and defend my existing supply centers. I will also prepare to counter Italian aggression in Tyrolia/Trieste and Russian aggression in Galicia. No specific joint moves have been agreed upon with England yet, but I intend to continue discussions for future cooperation.

[F1902M] F1902M was a catastrophic phase. The FGI+RT super-bloc launched a devastating, coordinated assault, resulting in the loss of Budapest, Bulgaria, Greece, Albania, and Galicia. Russia's seizure of Budapest, a home center, is a direct invasion. Italy aggressively expanded into Greece and Albania, while our fleet in Bulgaria was dislodged by Russia and Turkey. We are now reduced to just three supply centers: Vienna, Trieste, and a vacated Serbia. Our defensive strategy failed to secure Bohemia, which was lost. The hostile alliance is fully committed to our destruction. England is our sole potential ally, but immediate and decisive action is critical for our survival.

goals: Achieve world domination by controlling 18 supply centers.
Secure home supply centers (EDI, LON, LVP).
Defend home waters (ENG) against French aggression.
Counter the overwhelming Franco-German-Italian-Russian-Turkish super-alliance.
Secure builds in F1902.
Form a counter-alliance with Austria and other peripheral powers.
Exploit internal tensions and weaknesses within the super-alliance.
full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England and France both intend to move fleets to the English Channel (ENG), acknowledging a likely bounce but agreeing it will not lead to conflict, and are open to discussing cooperation in the Fall. A strong anti-German alliance has been formed with Russia, with mutual agreement to avoid conflict in the north (England's F EDI to NTH, Russia's F STP/SC to NOR) and to target Denmark (DEN) in Fall 1901. Germany plans F KIE to DEN and A BER to RUH, and is open to anti-French cooperation, which England is interested in exploring. Italy, Turkey, and Austria exchanged pleasantries with England, expressing general openness to cooperation.
Intent: F EDI - NTH (Fulfills agreement with Russia, non-conflicting with Germany's stated moves).
F LON - ENG (Fulfills stated intent to France, expecting a bounce but agreed not hostile).
A LVP - WAL (Secures a build, non-aggressive, keeps options open for future moves).

[S1901M] The ENG bounce with France was successful and non-hostile, confirming our agreement. My F EDI-NTH move also went as planned. However, Russia's F STP/SC held instead of moving to NOR, directly contradicting our anti-German alliance agreement. This is a significant betrayal, raising serious doubts about their commitment to targeting Denmark and our northern strategy. Germany successfully moved F KIE-DEN, making our planned Fall attack on DEN much harder without Russian support. I must immediately re-evaluate Russia's trustworthiness and their true intentions. This unexpected hold forces me to reconsider my Fall moves and potentially seek new alliances or adjust my anti-German strategy.

[F1901M] The anti-German alliance with Russia is back on track after Russia's apology for the S1901M oversight. We have agreed that Russia will move F STP/SC to NOR and England will move F NTH to HOL in F1901M, securing two builds and strengthening our northern front against Germany. France has confirmed their A BUR will move to BEL, securing Belgium. We discussed mutual expansion into the Low Countries, with England taking Holland and France taking Belgium, and agreed to maintain open communication for future cooperation. General pleasantries were exchanged with Austria, Turkey, and Italy, reinforcing a commitment to diplomacy.
Intent: F NTH - HOL (as agreed with Russia and discussed with France); F LON - ENG (hold, as discussed with France); A WAL - YOR (to secure a build). All agreed moves will be fulfilled.

[F1901M] Russia's complete betrayal in F1901M is unacceptable. Despite explicit agreements, they held all units, leaving my F NTH-HOL move to bounce against Germany and completely derailing our northern strategy. This is the second time Russia has proven utterly untrustworthy, and our "alliance" is now defunct. While the ENG bounce with France was successful and France secured Belgium as discussed, my planned expansion into Holland failed, and I only gained one build. Germany is now stronger, having secured Sweden and contested Holland. I must immediately abandon any reliance on Russia and re-evaluate my entire northern strategy, potentially seeking new partners against Germany or focusing elsewhere.

[S1902M] France has explicitly allied with Germany, declared support for Germany in Holland, and threatened England's home waters (F BRE-ENG). England views this as a betrayal and will defend its interests. Russia has repeatedly failed to uphold agreements, severely damaging trust. However, Russia has again apologized and promised to move F STP/SC-NOR and A LVN-PRU, and push into Galicia. England has given Russia one final chance to prove reliability. Germany is expanding into Sweden and contesting Holland, now bolstered by a French alliance.
Intent: F LON - ENG (to defend against France's declared F BRE-ENG); F NTH - DEN (to pressure Germany, leveraging Russia's promised NOR move); A YOR - WAL (for strategic flexibility and home defense). England will fulfill these stated intentions.

[S1902M] S1902M was a disaster. France's explicit support for Germany into Holland confirms their betrayal and the formation of a dangerous Franco-German alliance. Holland is now German, severely compromising our northern strategy. While we successfully defended ENG and secured Denmark, we are now isolated against two major powers.

Russia, predictably, betrayed us again by holding F STP/SC, rendering them completely untrustworthy and useless as an ally. We must abandon any hope of Russian cooperation. Our immediate priority is to consolidate our position, defend against the Franco-German threat, and seek new alliances. We need to secure builds and re-evaluate our entire strategy, focusing on survival and finding a way to break this new, formidable alliance.

[F1902M] The most critical development is the formation of an overwhelming Franco-German-Italian-Russian-Turkish (FGI+R+T) super-alliance, explicitly confirmed by multiple global statements. This bloc, claiming to seek 'stability,' is viewed by England and Austria as an aggressive concentration of power threatening the balance of Europe. Russia has definitively betrayed England, aligning with this super-alliance after repeated failures to uphold agreements. Turkey, despite initial overtures from England, has also joined this bloc. England is now isolated against this formidable alliance. Austria is under immense pressure from this super-alliance and is actively seeking concrete cooperation with England to counter the imbalance. England has consistently warned against the dangers of such a dominant bloc and attempted to sow discord within it, while seeking to form a counter-alliance with peripheral powers like Austria.
Intent: F DEN H (to secure Denmark as an English supply center and gain a build); F LON-ENG (to defend home waters against potential French aggression from BRE); A WAL S F LON-ENG (to support the defense of ENG).

[F1902M] F1902M confirms the overwhelming FGI+R+T super-alliance is fully active and coordinated. Critically, we lost Denmark to Germany, a devastating blow to our northern strategy and a direct result of Russia's repeated betrayals. This also corrects our previous misassessment of having "secured" Denmark. While we successfully defended ENG against France, this was a purely defensive victory. We are now isolated, holding only our three home centers, meaning no builds this year. Austria is under immense pressure, confirming their urgent need for a counter-alliance. Our immediate priority is survival, consolidating home waters, and forging a strong, active partnership with Austria to counter this formidable bloc.

goals: Achieve world domination by controlling 18 supply centers.
Expand aggressively, particularly into England and Austria.
Secure home centers.
Maintain and strengthen the expanded alliance with Germany, Italy, Russia, and Turkey.
Coordinate with Germany to pressure England (ENG, DEN).
Coordinate with Italy, Russia, and Turkey to pressure Austria (TYR, GRE, BUD, BOH, VIE).
Actively pressure England in ENG and support Germany's efforts against them.
Secure PIE this phase.
full_private_diary:
[S1901M] France has established a non-aggression pact and an alliance with Germany, agreeing to coordinate against England in the North Sea and English Channel. France will move F BRE to ENG, A PAR to BUR, and A MAR to SPA. Germany confirmed moves F KIE to DEN, A MUN to RUH, and A BER to PRU. France has also formed an alliance with Italy against Austria. France supports Italy's F NAP-ION-TUN plan, and Italy supports France's A MAR-SPA move. Italy confirmed moves F NAP to ION, A ROM to APU, and A VEN to TYR. Both France and Italy committed to coordinating Fall 1901 moves against Austria. With England, France agreed to a likely bounce in ENG, but both powers expressed a desire for future cooperation against Germany or in the Low Countries, and agreed the bounce doesn't necessitate conflict.
Intent: I intend to execute the following moves, fulfilling all agreements: F BRE to ENG, A MAR to SPA, and A PAR to BUR.

[S1901M] S1901M was a successful phase. All my planned moves executed as intended: F BRE-ENG (bounce), A MAR-SPA (success), and A PAR-BUR (success). Crucially, both Germany and Italy upheld their agreements, executing their promised moves. This reinforces their trustworthiness and the strength of our alliances. The expected bounce in ENG with England occurred, leaving open possibilities for future cooperation. My strategic position is excellent, having secured SPA and BUR for potential Fall builds. We are well-positioned to expand aggressively. The next step is to coordinate Fall 1901 moves with Italy against Austria and with Germany against England, leveraging our strong alliances for further gains.

[F1901M] Confirmed strong alliances with Germany and Italy. With Germany, agreed to coordinated F1901M moves: France A BUR-BEL, Germany A RUH-HOL, Germany F DEN-SWE. France will move F BRE-ENG (likely bounce). Agreed to plan a stronger S1902 assault on England. With Italy, agreed to coordinated F1901M moves against Austria: France A SPA-POR, Italy F ION-TUN, A TYR-TRI, A APU-VEN. With England, confirmed a non-hostile bounce in ENG and France informed England of A BUR-BEL; England acknowledged and expressed continued interest in future cooperation. Exchanged general diplomatic pleasantries with Austria, Russia, and Turkey.
Intent: F BRE - ENG (Fulfilling agreement with Germany to keep pressure on England, and with England for a non-hostile bounce). A SPA - POR (Fulfilling agreement with Italy to secure a new SC). A BUR - BEL (Fulfilling agreement with Germany to secure a new SC, and informed England).

[F1901M] F1901M was a highly successful phase. All my planned moves executed, securing BEL and POR, bringing my SC count to five. Crucially, both Germany and Italy proved to be reliable allies, executing all agreed moves and making significant gains (Germany took SWE, Italy took TUN, TRI, VEN). This strengthens our alliances and confirms their trustworthiness. The expected non-hostile bounce in ENG with England occurred. However, England's F NTH-HOL move, bouncing my ally Germany, is a significant development. This direct aggression against Germany complicates my previously friendly stance with England and demands a re-evaluation of our future cooperation. Russia's complete passivity was also unexpected. I am well-positioned for further aggressive expansion.

[S1902M] France has solidified its alliances with Germany and Italy. England's F NTH-HOL move against Germany in F1901M led to a complete breakdown of Anglo-French relations, with England explicitly declaring France an 'enemy' due to France's commitment to Germany. France has agreed to actively support Germany's A RUH-HOL move with A BEL S A RUH - HOL, and will move F BRE - ENG to pressure England. Germany confirmed A RUH - HOL, F KIE S A RUH - HOL, A SIL - BOH, and F SWE H. France also confirmed A PAR - BUR, F MAR - LYO, and A POR - SPA. With Italy, France reaffirmed its alliance against Austria. Italy outlined its S1902M plans: A VEN - TYR (supporting Germany's A SIL - BOH), A TRI - ALB (supporting Turkey's A BUL - GRE), F TUN - ION, F NAP - TYS, and A ROM - APU. France confirmed its moves (A PAR - BUR, F MAR - LYO, A POR - SPA) align with supporting Italy's broader strategy against Austria.
Intent: I intend to execute the following moves, fulfilling all agreements: F BRE - ENG (to pressure England, agreed with Germany), A BEL S A RUH - HOL (to secure Holland for Germany, agreed with Germany), A PAR - BUR (to expand influence and pressure Austria, agreed with Germany and Italy), F MAR - LYO (to position for Mediterranean control and support Italy, agreed with Germany and Italy), and A POR - SPA (to secure a new supply center, agreed with Germany and Italy).

[S1902M] S1902M was a strong phase for France and our allies. We successfully secured SPA, bringing our total to 6 supply centers, and expanded into BUR and LYO. Our coordinated efforts with Germany paid off, securing Holland for them. Italy also made good progress, expanding into ION, ALB, TYS, and APU, demonstrating the strength of our alliance.

However, Austria proved more resilient than anticipated. Their unexpected move into TYR bounced both Germany's attack on BOH and Italy's support, halting our planned advance. This requires a re-evaluation of our strategy against Austria. England's attempt to move into ENG, bouncing our fleet, confirms their hostile intent. We must continue to pressure England while devising a more effective plan to break Austria's defenses.

[F1902M] France has solidified a powerful alliance with Germany, Italy, Russia, and Turkey, primarily targeting England and Austria. With Germany, France confirmed F BRE-ENG, A BEL-PIC, and A BUR-GAS to pressure England, while Germany will move F KIE-DEN (supported by F SWE) and A SIL will support Russia's A GAL-BUD. With Italy, France confirmed F LYO-PIE, which Italy's F TYS will support. Italy will also move A VEN-TYR and A ALB-GRE (supported by F ION and Turkey's F AEG). Italy is coordinating with Germany and Russia for further pushes into Bohemia, Vienna, and Budapest. England and Austria are openly hostile, viewing the F-G-I-R-T alliance as an aggressive threat to the balance of power. Russia and Turkey have explicitly joined the alliance and are coordinating against Austria.
Intent: I intend to execute the following moves, fulfilling all agreements: F BRE - ENG (to pressure England), A BEL - PIC (to open options on England's flank), A BUR - GAS (to open options on England's flank), and F LYO - PIE (to pressure Austria from the west, supported by Italy).

[F1902M] F1902M saw France successfully expand, securing PIE, PIC, and GAS, bringing our supply center count to 7. Our F BRE-ENG move bounced, confirming England's hostility, but their capture of SKA from Germany is a significant concern, strengthening them in the north. While Italy's F TYS and Germany's A SIL deviated from agreed support, our core objectives were met, and the alliance remains robust. Russia and Turkey made excellent progress against Austria, securing BUD and BUL/EC. Austria, however, proved resilient in TYR, bouncing Italy's attack. We must now prioritize containing England's northern expansion and devising a more decisive plan to break Austria's defenses, ensuring all agreed supports are executed moving forward.

goals: Achieve world domination by controlling 18 supply centers.
Execute a decisive F1902M assault against Austria (A SIL S A GAL-BUD, coordinated with Russia and Italy's moves).
Reclaim DEN from England in F1902M (F KIE-DEN, supported by F SWE).
Maintain strong alliances with France, Russia, and Italy.
Continue to weaken Austria and neutralize England as primary threats.
full_private_diary:
[S1901M] Confirmed S1901M moves: F KIE-DEN, A MUN-RUH, A BER-PRU with Austria, France, Russia, and Italy. Established a non-aggression pact with France and discussed coordinating against England (France moving F BRE-ENG). Discussed cooperation with England against France (England moving F EDI-NTH). Established strong interest in an alliance with Russia against Austria, identifying Galicia and Bohemia as Fall targets. Russia shared F STP-NOR, F SEV-RUM, A WAR-UKR, A MOS-LVN. Maintained good relations with Austria (no border conflict, Austria moving A VIE-GAL, F TRI-ALB, A BUD-SER). Maintained good relations with Italy (no conflict, Italy moving F NAP-ION, A ROM-APU, A VEN-TYR, noting A VEN-TYR's strategic implications for Austria).
Intent: Execute agreed-upon moves: F KIE to DEN, A MUN to RUH, A BER to PRU. These moves secure neutral territories and maintain flexibility for future alliances, particularly regarding the conflicting interests with England and France, and the potential alliance with Russia against Austria.

[S1901M] S1901M was a strong start. All German moves (F KIE-DEN, A MUN-RUH, A BER-PRU) succeeded as planned, securing three crucial supply centers and expanding our power. This confirms the reliability of our initial diplomatic efforts.

The expected conflict between England and France materialized, with both F BRE-ENG and F LON-ENG bouncing. This is a positive development, as it keeps them engaged with each other and preserves our flexibility to align with either. England's F EDI-NTH move was successful, as discussed. Russia's moves (F SEV-RUM, A WAR-UKR, A MOS-LVN) align perfectly with our proposed alliance against Austria, positioning them well for future action. Austria and Italy executed their discussed moves without conflict. Our position is excellent, with all options open.

[F1901M] Germany has finalized F1901M plans with France, Italy, and Russia. An anti-Austrian alliance (Germany, Italy, Russia) is confirmed: Germany will move A PRU-SIL, Italy will move F ION-TUN, A TYR-TRI, A APU-VEN, and Russia will hold RUM, UKR, LVN to consolidate for a decisive S1902 blow. An anti-England alliance (Germany, France) is also confirmed: Germany will move F DEN-SWE and A RUH-HOL, while France will move A BUR-BEL and F BRE-ENG (expecting a bounce). Both Germany and France agree to a stronger, combined assault on England in S1902. Germany will secure HOL and SWE this Fall.
Intent: I intend to fulfill all agreed-upon moves: F DEN-SWE, A RUH-HOL, and A PRU-SIL. These moves secure two neutral supply centers (SWE, HOL) and directly pressure Austria's heartland (SIL), aligning with my alliances.

[F1901M] F1901M was a mixed but largely successful phase. The anti-Austrian alliance with Italy and Russia executed flawlessly: I secured SIL, Italy gained TUN, TRI, VEN, and Russia held their positions. Austria is now severely weakened, and we are well-positioned for a decisive S1902 strike. My F DEN-SWE move also succeeded, securing a crucial supply center.

However, England proved untrustworthy. Despite our agreement with France to contain them, England moved F NTH-HOL, bouncing my A RUH-HOL and denying me a key supply center. This reveals England's hostile intent towards me, forcing a re-evaluation of my western strategy. France did secure BEL, strengthening our western front. I must now prioritize securing HOL and dealing with England's aggression, while maintaining pressure on Austria.

[S1902M] Germany has secured strong alliances with France, Russia, and Italy for S1902M. The primary focus is a decisive two-front assault: 1) Against Austria, with Germany (A SIL-BOH, supported by Italy's A VEN-TYR), Russia (A UKR-GAL, A WAR S A UKR-GAL, A LVN-PRU, F STP/SC-NOR), and Italy (A TRI-ALB supporting Turkey's attack on F GRE). All parties are fully aligned for this. 2) Against England, in response to their F NTH-HOL move in F1901M, which bounced Germany's unit. Germany (A RUH-HOL, supported by F KIE) and France (A BEL S A RUH-HOL, F BRE-ENG, A POR-SPA, A PAR-BUR, F MAR-LYO) are fully aligned to secure Holland and pressure England. Austria is aware of Turkey's aggression and is focusing on the south-east, while still attempting to maintain 'friendly' relations with Germany, unaware of the coordinated attack.
Intent: I intend to fulfill all agreed-upon moves for S1902M: A RUH - HOL (supported by France's A BEL and my F KIE), A SIL - BOH (supported by Italy's A VEN-TYR), F KIE S A RUH - HOL, and F SWE H.

[S1902M] S1902M brought mixed results. Our alliance with France proved effective, securing HOL with their crucial support, a vital gain. Russia also delivered, successfully taking GAL and PRU, significantly weakening Austria. However, our push into BOH was unexpectedly thwarted; Austria's A BOH-TYR bounced Italy's support, preventing our A SIL-BOH. This means Austria is more resilient than anticipated.

The most concerning development is England's aggression. While France failed to dislodge them from ENG, England seized DEN, directly threatening our northern flank and expanding their power. This confirms England as a primary and growing threat. We must adjust our anti-Austrian strategy to account for their resilience and prioritize countering England's expansion, potentially with French naval assistance.

[F1902M] Germany, Russia, and Italy have coordinated a decisive F1902M assault against Austria. Germany's A SIL will support Russia's A GAL-BUD. Russia will move A GAL-BUD and F RUM-BUL/EC. Italy will move A VEN-TYR and A ALB-GRE (supported by F ION). Austria is aware of this large, hostile alliance and is openly complaining. On the western front, Germany and France are coordinating against England. Germany's F KIE will move to DEN, supported by F SWE, to reclaim Denmark from England. Turkey also respected our spheres, moving into Armenia and attempting to move into Serbia, avoiding Greece. The primary concern is Austria's successful capture of Greece, which directly threatens our Balkan ambitions. This reinforces Austria as our immediate enemy. We must now coordinate closely with our allies to counter Austria's strengthened position in the Balkans and secure our next objectives. Trust in our alliances remains high.

Russia betrayed our agreement by holding F RUM, failing to support Turkey's A BUL-GRE. This allowed Austria to successfully move F GRE-BUL/SC, gaining a critical supply center and strengthening its Balkan presence. Turkey's A BUL-GRE bounced, and Greece remains Austrian. Germany's A SIL-BOH also bounced against Austria. Russia's unreliability is a serious concern, and the Balkan strategy needs immediate re-evaluation. We must address Austria's new strength in Bulgaria and Russia's broken promise, as this significantly impacts our eastern expansion.

Russia will move A GAL-BUD (supported by Germany's A SIL) and F RUM-BUL/EC (supported by Turkey's F BLA). Russia clarified its F RUM hold in S1902M was an error, reaffirming commitment to the alliance. Germany will also move F KIE-DEN (supported by F SWE) to reclaim Denmark. My own error in holding F TYS instead of supporting France is regrettable, though France's move still succeeded. More concerning is Germany's failure to support Russia's A GAL-BUD, raising questions about their commitment or communication. While we're making progress against Austria, these coordination issues and Austria's strong counter-attacks demand immediate attention and a re-evaluation of our northern strategy.

[S1901M] Turkey has established strong diplomatic ties for S1901M. A non-aggression pact (NAP) has been agreed with Italy, establishing clear spheres of influence: Italy to Greece and the Western Mediterranean, Turkey to Syria, Armenia, and the Black Sea. Both powers shared their Spring moves, and agreed to potential future cooperation against Austria or Russia. A NAP for the Black Sea has been agreed with Russia, and both shared their Spring moves. Turkey and Russia also agreed to discuss cooperation against Austria in the Balkans for Fall 1901. A NAP for shared border regions has been agreed with Austria, and both shared their Spring moves. Austria also informed Turkey of Russia's F SEV-ROM move. General pleasantries were exchanged with England, Germany, and France.
Intent: My specific intent for S1901M is to move F ANK to Black Sea, A CON to Bulgaria, and A SMY to Syria. These moves are in full accordance with the agreements made with Italy and Russia, and I intend to fulfill all agreed-upon commitments.

[S1901M] S1901M was a resounding success. All my planned moves, F ANK-BLA, A CON-BUL, and A SMY-SYR, were executed without opposition, securing two vital neutral supply centers and control of the Black Sea. Crucially, the non-aggression pacts with Italy and Russia held firm, demonstrating their trustworthiness for this phase. Italy's moves were as expected, and Russia honored our Black Sea agreement. I noted Russia's F SEV-RUM move, which differed from Austria's earlier report of F SEV-ROM, but this did not affect my plans. My strategic position is excellent, having achieved all immediate goals and confirmed reliable partners. I will now focus on consolidating these gains and preparing for potential cooperation with Russia against Austria in the Balkans for Fall 1901, as previously discussed.

[F1901M] Turkey successfully maintained non-aggression pacts with Italy and Russia. Italy confirmed Greece as its sphere of influence, leading Turkey to adjust its A BUL move from Greece to Serbia, which Italy enthusiastically supported as a direct pressure on Austria. Russia also understood and supported Turkey's adjusted move to Serbia, reaffirming its commitment to the anti-Austrian alliance and promising direct support in S1902. Austria was informed of Turkey's focus on the Levant and Aegean, and while disappointed about no direct help in Romania, maintained friendly relations. General pleasantries were exchanged with England, Germany, and France.
Intent: My specific intent for F1901M is to move A SYR - ARM, A BUL - SER, and F BLA S A BUL - SER. These moves are fully aligned with agreements made with Italy and Russia, and I intend to fulfill all agreed-upon commitments.

[F1901M] F1901M yielded mixed results. I successfully secured Armenia, expanding into the Levant as planned. However, my push into Serbia was bounced by a strong Austrian defense, who also gained Greece. This was a surprise given our 'friendly relations' and their stated focus elsewhere. More concerning was Russia's complete inaction; despite verbal support for our anti-Austrian move, all their units held. This seriously questions their commitment to our alliance and their trustworthiness for immediate cooperation. I also made a critical error by ordering F BLA H instead of supporting A BUL-SER, which could have secured Serbia. Austria is now stronger in the Balkans, and Russia's reliability is suspect. I must re-evaluate my Balkan strategy and alliances for S1902.

[S1902M] Negotiations confirm a complete breakdown of relations with Austria, who now considers Turkey an enemy due to the attempted Serbia move. Turkey reciprocates this hostile stance. A strong alliance with Italy and Russia against Austria has been solidified. Italy will move A TRI-ALB to support Turkey's A BUL-GRE move. Russia has confirmed F RUM S A BUL-GRE. Turkey's other moves are A ARM-SYR and F SMY-AEG. The Black Sea non-aggression pact with Russia remains firm, with F BLA holding.
Intent: My specific intent for S1902M is to move A BUL-GRE, supported by Italy's A TRI-ALB and Russia's F RUM. I will also move A ARM-SYR and F SMY-AEG. F BLA will hold to maintain the Black Sea non-aggression pact with Russia. All agreed-upon moves will be fulfilled.

[S1902M] S1902M was a critical setback in the Balkans. Russia's F RUM held instead of supporting my A BUL-GRE, a direct betrayal that allowed Austria to take Bulgaria and hold Greece. This confirms Russia's untrustworthiness, as this is the second time they've failed to support my anti-Austrian moves. Italy, however, proved reliable, moving A TRI-ALB as promised. Austria is now significantly stronger in the Balkans, controlling both Greece and Bulgaria, making my expansion there much harder. On the positive side, I successfully secured Syria and the Aegean. My anti-Austrian strategy, reliant on Russia, is now in tatters. I must re-evaluate my alliances and consider Russia a hostile power.

[F1902M] Turkey has confirmed a coordinated attack on Greece with Italy: Italy's A ALB will move to GRE, supported by Italy's F ION and Turkey's F AEG. Turkey has also agreed to support Russia's F RUM-BUL/EC move with F BLA, despite severe distrust due to Russia's previous betrayals. Russia has apologized for its past 'error' and reaffirmed commitment to the anti-Austrian alliance and Black Sea NAP. Turkey will move A SYR to ARM to secure a neutral center and A CON will hold. England has initiated contact, expressing shared concerns about the growing Franco-German-Italian bloc and Italy's aggression in the Balkans, to which Turkey has responded positively, indicating openness to discussion.
Intent: F AEG S A ALB - GRE (Italy's unit) - Fulfill agreement with Italy. F BLA S F RUM - BUL/EC (Russia's unit) - Fulfill agreement with Russia, with extreme caution. A SYR - ARM - Secure neutral territory. A CON H - Secure home territory.

[F1902M] F1902M was a resounding success, dramatically reversing the setbacks of S1902M. Our coordinated attack with Italy on Greece was perfectly executed, with Italy securing the center. Crucially, Russia followed through on its promise, moving F RUM-BUL/EC, supported by my F BLA, dislodging Austria and securing Bulgaria. This is a massive blow to Austria, who has now lost both Greece and Bulgaria. I successfully secured Armenia, expanding my influence. Italy has proven to be a steadfast and reliable ally. Russia's actions this phase, while welcome, do not erase past betrayals; I will remain cautious but acknowledge their current cooperation. The Balkan situation has shifted decisively in our favor, weakening Austria significantly and opening new avenues for expansion.

goals: Achieve world domination by controlling 18 supply centers.
Survive the coordinated aggression of the Franco-German-Italian-Russian-Turkish super-bloc.
Secure all Austrian home centers (BUD, VIE) and existing supply centers (GRE, SER, BUL).
